mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending earnings declines, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening corporate output capacity, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery phases, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly different from zero (t-stat < 1.5) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms sustained economic activity and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below -0.02 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in <50% of high-payroll-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration above 0.4% monthly forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields negative predictive power, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of cases where both series expand simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle peak signals capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle production declines, or the signal loses significance when filtered for pre-recession quarters.
